regions.name: Wenatchee Mountains
regions.character: Volcanic rock, loose talus, ridge exposure, afternoon storms

regions.name: Chelan-Sawtooth
regions.character: Stable talus, loose scree descent, trail hazards

regions.name: East Cascades
regions.character: Granite rock, stream crossings, wet sections

regions.name: Enchantments
regions.character: Notably loose talus, weather isolation, alpine terrain

regions.name: North Cascades Foothills
regions.character: Lower elevation, afternoon storms, popularity varies

regions.name: Stuart Range
regions.character: Non-technical alternatives to technical alpine routes

regions.name: Pasayten Wilderness
regions.character: Remote wilderness, sparse route markers, avalanche terrain

regions.name: Alpine Lakes Wilderness
regions.character: Long approach, alpine lake basins, navigation critical

regions.name: North Cascades (High Alpine)
regions.character: CRITICAL: Crevasses, altitude, limited season (1-2 weeks)

class_2: Crest Lake Peak - East Side Scramble
Source Lake Peak - Northeast Ridge Route
class_2_plus: Matterhorn Pass Peak - East Slope Approach
class_2_3: Glacier Peak - Cool Glacier Non-Technical Approach

routes_by_elevation.name: Glacier Peak - Cool Glacier Non-Technical Approach
Source Lake Peak - Northeast Ridge Route

july: All routes accessible, early summer conditions
august: Peak season for all routes, best rock conditions
september: Excellent conditions, some high peaks getting early snow
october: Late season possible for lower peaks

most_dangerous: Glacier Peak crevasses (CRITICAL - requires rope skills despite 'scrambling' classification)
Steep ridge exposure (Independence Peak, Colchuck Peak, cathedral Peak)
Remote wilderness rescue impossibility (Pasayten, Glacier Peak)
Weather window extreme limitation (Glacier Peak - 1-2 weeks only)
hazard_summary.most_common_descent_hazard: Talus/scree requiring controlled descent with trekking poles

beginner_alpine_scramblers.description: Pure Class 2, minimal exposure, good training
recommended: Crest Lake Peak - East Side Scramble
Jack Mountain - North Cascades Foothills Route

intermediate_alpine_scramblers.description: Class 2-3 terrain, moderate exposure, hands required
recommended: Miners Ridge Pass Peak - West Face Scramble

advanced_alpine_scramblers.description: Class 2-3 with significant exposure, steep terrain, routefinding challenge
recommended: Matterhorn Pass Peak - East Slope Approach
Glacier Peak - Cool Glacier Non-Technical (CRITICAL SKILLS: ROPE + GLACIER)

crowded_avoidance.description: Less-visited peaks with good training value
recommended: Source Lake Peak - Northeast Ridge Route
Matterhorn Pass Peak - East Slope Approach (very remote)
Miners Ridge Pass Peak - West Face Scramble

early_season.description: Lower elevation, south-facing, snow melt early
recommended: Teneriffe Mountain - East Slope Route (5,029 ft)
Whitehorse Mountain - South Ridge Route (6,994 ft)
Snoqualmie Peak - West Slope Scramble (6,278 ft)
